candidates.name: ICIJ Offshore Leaks Database (Panama/Paradise/Pandora/Bahamas/Offshore Leaks) — CSV + Neo4j bulk
candidates.bridges: Four node types (Entities, Officers, Intermediaries, Addresses) + all_edges file linking them. No hard registry IDs, so it bridges purely on OFFICER NAME and ADDRESS — the creative join that pierces shell-company anonymity.
candidates.de_silos: Connects to the warehouse's huge NAME (432) and ADDRESS (422 carriers) soft-key surface, and to OpenSanctions (which republishes ICIJ as a source). Officer/address overlap is the bridge from offshore shells to onshore EIN/CIK/registry entities sharing the same beneficial owner or registered agent.
candidates.access: Free bulk, no auth. CSV zip (full-oldb.LATEST.zip) + Neo4j v4/v5 dumps. ODbL (structure) + CC-BY-SA (contents), attribution to ICIJ required.
candidates.volume: >810,000 offshore entities, links across 200+ countries, ~80 years to 2020; millions of edge rows
candidates.creative_join: The canonical address/officer-name de-anonymizer: a shared registered address or a repeated officer name links a US EIN nonprofit or SEC filer to an offshore shell with no common hard ID. This is the highest-payoff NAME/ADDRESS bridge in the warehouse.

candidates.name: UK Companies House — Persons with Significant Control (PSC) Snapshot bulk
candidates.bridges: Maps every UK company (company_number) to its beneficial owners/controllers by NAME + ADDRESS + DOB + nationality, including corporate PSCs (company-owns-company edges). Pairs with the free CH 'Basic Company Data' product to add registration/SIC info.
candidates.de_silos: Bridges UK corporate entities into the NAME/ADDRESS clusters and, via corporate-PSC chains, into OpenOwnership and OpenSanctions gb_coh_psc. UK company_number is also the join key for OpenCorporates UK rows and the GLEIF OC-to-LEI file.
candidates.access: Free, no auth. JSON snapshot, single large file or split files, overwritten daily before 10am GMT. URL template: download.companieshouse.gov.uk/persons-with-significant-control-snapshot-YYYY-MM-DD.zip. Open Government Licence.
candidates.volume: ~10M+ PSC statements across ~5M UK companies
candidates.creative_join: Corporate-PSC edges (a company controlled by another company) build cross-border ownership chains; matching PSC person name+DOB+address against ICIJ officers and OpenSanctions PEPs surfaces UK shells fronting for sanctioned/offshore actors.

verdict.name: UK Companies House — Persons with Significant Control (PSC) Snapshot bulk
verdict.access_reality: Confirmed at download.companieshouse.gov.uk/en_pscdata.html. Free, no auth, JSON, updated daily before 10am GMT, available as one file or 32 segments (10-69MB each). Page states 'provided free of charge and will not be supported.' License is Open Government Licence (standard for CH bulk products). Field list (company_number, PSC name, address, partial DOB, nationality, corporate-PSC kind) is documented on the CH developer pages — corporate-PSC edges confirmed by the PSC data model.
verdict.notes: company_number is a STRONG (not STEEL) ID — new token for the tagger (UK_COMPANY_NUMBER). Bridges into OpenCorporates UK rows and the GLEIF OC-to-LEI file by company_number, and into OpenOwnership/OpenSanctions gb_coh_psc. PSC person identity is name+partial-DOB+address (probabilistic across datasets). Corporate-PSC chains give cross-border ownership edges. Solid as a standalone landing; cross-dataset person matching is probabilistic.

candidates.name: Open Ownership — Beneficial Ownership Register bulk (BODS) + republished BO datasets
candidates.bridges: BODS statements link people to entities via ownership/control across Denmark, Slovakia, UK (and republished registers), carrying entity_identifiers that include LEI and national company numbers — a person<->company<->LEI bridge in standardized form.
candidates.de_silos: Connects to GLEIF via the OpenOwnership-Register-ID-to-LEI mapping file (candidate 2), to UK PSC (candidate 5) by company_number, and to the NAME/ADDRESS soft-key clusters. The standardized person_identifiers table is a cross-jurisdiction owner-name spine.
candidates.access: Free, Open Data Commons Attribution License. ~10GB bulk, structured BODS JSON tables (statements, person_names, person_addresses, entity_identifiers, etc). NOTE: the live Register was closed Nov 2024; republished datasets remain downloadable via bods-data.openownership.org.
candidates.volume: ~10GB of statements; millions of ownership/person/entity records
candidates.creative_join: The entity_identifiers table is itself a mini-crosswalk (national company number <-> LEI). Combined with the GLEIF OO-to-LEI file it deterministically wires beneficial-owner people into the LEI ownership tree — owner-name de-anonymization with a hard-ID anchor.

candidates.name: OpenCorporates — Company + Officer data (Snowflake Marketplace sample + open-data bulk/API)
candidates.bridges: Largest open legal-entity DB: every company keyed by jurisdiction_code+company_number, with an officers table (officer name -> companies). Bridges to LEI via the GLEIF OpenCorporates-ID-to-LEI file, and to UK PSC / national registers via company_number.
candidates.de_silos: Officer-name graph is the cross-registry de-anonymizer (same officer across US-state, UK, EU companies). Its OpenCorporates-ID is the explicit join key in the GLEIF OC-to-LEI crosswalk, wiring the whole OpenCorporates corpus into the LEI ownership tree. Sample data is on Snowflake Marketplace — native to the Ripple stack, zero ingestion friction.
candidates.access: Bulk delivery + API (JSON/XML); free for open-data projects under open licence; sample files free on Snowflake Marketplace. Full corpus is gated/paid for commercial — flag COST and AUTH_REQUIRED. No D&B acquisition confirmed (OpenCorporates remains independent as of 2025).
candidates.volume: 200M+ companies across 140+ jurisdictions; tens of millions of officer records
candidates.creative_join: The officer table turns 'same name' into 'same person directing N companies across N jurisdictions' — the directorship bridge. Because Ripple is Snowflake-native, the Marketplace listing means this can be wired via a share rather than a load.

clusters.thesis: person<->org<->money bridges (lobbying / FARA / campaign finance / disclosure)
clusters.bridge_thesis: Ripple's hard-ID backbone is healthcare-only (NPI/CCN) and its corporate/contractor islands (USASpending subawards, SEC, foreign registries) connect by coarse keys; this cluster injects NAMED PEOPLE — donors, lobbyists, foreign agents, members of Congress — and the employer/affiliation fields that wire those people to the companies and committees Ripple already holds, while the congress crosswalk converts name-piles into hard legislator IDs.

candidates.name: @unitedstates/congress-legislators (BIOGUIDE<->FEC<->ICPSR<->OpenSecrets<->GovTrack crosswalk)
candidates.bridges: BIOGUIDE<->FEC_CAND_ID<->ICPSR<->OpenSecrets ID — the Rosetta Stone for every member of Congress, 1789-present; turns a legislator NAME into 10+ hard IDs at once
candidates.de_silos: Wires the unitedstates/congress crosswalk (already scouted) to FEC bulk (committees/candidates) and to any vote/bill data; the single file that lets a donor->committee->candidate chain resolve to a named, ID'd legislator
candidates.access: GitHub raw / API; no auth; free; no rate limit; YAML + JSON + CSV (legislators-current.csv, legislators-historical.csv via scripts/alternate_bulk_formats.py)
candidates.volume: ~12,500 legislators (current + historical) + committees + executives; few MB
candidates.creative_join: FEC_CAND_ID here is the linchpin: join it to FEC candidate master, then FEC individual-contributions EMPLOYER field maps a member's donor base back to companies Ripple already holds by NAME/EIN — person becomes the bridge between campaign money and corporate data

verdict.name: @unitedstates/congress-legislators (BIOGUIDE<->FEC<->ICPSR<->OpenSecrets<->GovTrack crosswalk)
verdict.access_reality: Fetched the live legislators-current.yaml. Confirmed verbatim id block carries bioguide, thomas, lis, govtrack, opensecrets, votesmart, fec (array), cspan, wikipedia, house_history, ballotpedia, maplight, icpsr, wikidata, google_entity_id, pictorial. GitHub raw / API, no auth, free, no enforced rate limit on raw files. CSV variants (legislators-current.csv) generated via scripts/alternate_bulk_formats.py - note CSV is a derived artifact, the YAML/JSON are canonical. Default branch is 'main' and the URL resolves.
verdict.notes: This is the genuine Rosetta Stone of the cluster - one file turns a legislator NAME into 10+ hard IDs. The fec field is an ARRAY (one member can have multiple FEC candidate IDs across House/Senate runs), so the loader must explode it, not treat it as scalar. opensecrets id (N-prefix) and icpsr are both present and are the real cross-domain levers. Few MB. Highest-leverage candidate here; correctly flagged scouted_not_loaded.

candidates.name: FEC Bulk Data — Individual Contributions + Candidate-Committee Linkage + Committee/Candidate Master
candidates.bridges: EMPLOYER/OCCUPATION free-text on every donation ties a named PERSON to the company they work for; CAND_ID<->CMTE_ID linkage file bridges money to candidates; CMTE_ID bridges to lobbying LD-203 contributions
candidates.de_silos: New island -> connects to congress-legislators (FEC_CAND_ID) and, via the EMPLOYER text field fuzzy-matched to NAME, to Ripple's corporate carriers (NAME 432, ADDRESS 422); donor ZIP joins to the ZIP backbone (5,541 edges)
candidates.access: Bulk ZIP download (cnYY.zip, cmYY.zip, cclYY.zip, indivYY.zip, oth.zip); no auth; free; pipe-delimited TXT; per-cycle files back to 1980
candidates.volume: Individual contributions ~70M+ rows per recent 2-yr cycle; multi-GB unzipped
candidates.creative_join: The EMPLOYER field is the creative cross-domain lever: it is an uncontrolled NAME of an org, so a normalized-NAME join lights up donor->employer->Ripple corporate/contractor entity; pairs with USASpending recipients to ask 'did this contractor's executives donate to the members who fund the contract?'

verdict.name: FEC Bulk Data — Individual Contributions + Candidate-Committee Linkage + Committee/Candidate Master
verdict.access_reality: Bulk portal at fec.gov/data/browse-data/?tab=bulk-data; the /files/bulk-downloads/ path 302-redirects to a cloud.gov S3 bucket (s3-us-gov-west-1) - downloads are free, no auth, but the naive deep-link URL in the candidate (indiv24.zip) is served via redirect, so the loader must follow redirects. Files are ZIP-of-pipe-delimited TXT (itcont.txt etc.), LF line endings, per 2-yr cycle back to ~1980. Confirmed: indiv (individual contributions), ccl (candidate-committee linkage), cm (committee master), cn (candidate master).
verdict.notes: Real and large. indiv file is genuinely ~70M+ rows/cycle, multi-GB unzipped - volume is real, plan for chunked load. CRITICAL gotcha: bulk indiv files have NO header row and are pipe-delimited; column names come from a separate header/data-dictionary file (indiv_header_file.csv) - must map columns manually. EMPLOYER/OCCUPATION are uncontrolled free-text (the creative join), so any NAME/EMPLOYER->corporate match is fuzzy/probabilistic, not a hard join. FEC_CAND_ID is the clean linchpin into congress-legislators. ccl is the bridge that turns committee money into candidate money.

candidates.name: U.S. Senate LDA Lobbying Disclosure — REST API + bulk LD-1/LD-2/LD-203
candidates.publisher: Office of the Secretary of the U.S. Senate / Clerk of the House (LDA.gov)
candidates.bridges: Ties a named LOBBYIST (person) to a REGISTRANT (lobbying firm) to a CLIENT (the company paying); LD-203 contribution reports carry FEC committee references — bridges lobbying to campaign-finance committees; many lobbyists are former members (cross to congress crosswalk by NAME)
candidates.de_silos: New island -> client/registrant NAME+ADDRESS join to Ripple's corporate NAME/ADDRESS carriers and USASpending recipients; LD-203 FEC_CMTE_ID joins to FEC bulk; lobbyist NAME bridges to congress-legislators (revolving door)
candidates.access: REST API (token, free registration) + bulk quarterly downloads (XML/CSV by quarter); migrating to LDA.gov after 06/30/2026 — update endpoint; JSON
candidates.volume: 1M+ filings since 1999; ~50-70k filings/yr
candidates.creative_join: Revolving-door join: match LD-2 lobbyist NAME (the file flags former-member status) against congress-legislators historical YAML to surface ex-Congress members now lobbying the body they served, then chain client->Ripple corporate entity — a person-as-bridge story no single dataset tells

verdict.name: U.S. Senate LDA Lobbying Disclosure — REST API + bulk LD-1/LD-2/LD-203
verdict.access_reality: lda.senate.gov/api/ live. REST API requires a free API key (register for key, no cost). Confirmed endpoints/data: LD-1/LD-2 filings, LD-203 contributions (data from 2008-07-30), and registrant/client ID lookup. MIGRATION CONFIRMED: explicit banner 'After 06/30/2026 this site will no longer be available' - directs to new LDA.gov platform and to update REST API saved links. That deadline is 3 days from the current date (2026-06-27), so the lda.senate.gov endpoint is about to break - the loader MUST target the new LDA.gov base URL.
verdict.notes: Real and high-value, but the endpoint in the candidate (lda.senate.gov/api/) is days from decommission - retarget to LDA.gov before building or it breaks on first run. Keys confirmed: REGISTRANT_ID/CLIENT_ID, lobbyist NAME, ADDRESS, ISSUE_CODE. FEC_CMTE_ID on LD-203: LD-203 carries contribution recipient data but the FEC committee ID is not always cleanly populated as a structured field - treat FEC_CMTE_ID linkage as probable-not-guaranteed (hence 'likely' not 'confirmed'). NAICS is NOT a native LDA field - lobbying uses its own ISSUE_CODE taxonomy, so drop the NAICS claim. Free, JSON, rate-limited per token.

candidates.name: DOJ FARA Bulk Data — Registrants + Foreign Principals + Short Forms
candidates.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, National Security Division (FARA Unit)
candidates.bridges: Foreign-principal file ties a named foreign government/company (COUNTRY + NAME) to a US REGISTRANT (firm/person acting as agent); short-form file names the individual agents — a person<->foreign-org<->US-org triple
candidates.de_silos: New island -> COUNTRY key joins to Ripple's COUNTRY backbone and foreign company registries (Zefix, BORME, GEMI, CRO islands); registrant NAME/ADDRESS joins to US corporate + Senate-LDA carriers (FARA registrants are frequently the same lobbying firms)
candidates.access: Bulk CSV/XML ZIP, daily refresh, ISO-8859-1; no auth; free; also V1 API (CSV/XML/JSON)
candidates.volume: 80,000+ filings; registrants + principals tables tens of thousands of rows
candidates.creative_join: Cross-dataset entity join: a FARA registrant NAME that also appears in Senate-LDA as a domestic lobbying firm exposes who lobbies for foreign principals AND domestic clients; foreign-principal COUNTRY+NAME bridges to the dormant foreign-registry islands, turning 'agent of X' into a hard corporate record

verdict.name: DOJ FARA Bulk Data — Registrants + Foreign Principals + Short Forms
verdict.access_reality: efile.fara.gov bulk facility confirmed live. Working direct file e.g. https://efile.fara.gov/bulk/zip/FARA_All_ForeignPrincipals.csv.zip and bulk index at efile.fara.gov/ords/fara/f?p=107:21. Bulk in CSV or XML, updated DAILY, free, no auth. The candidate's URL (f?p=API:BULKDATA) is an older/alternate ORDS app path but the facility resolves; safer to point at the direct /bulk/zip/ CSV files. Datasets include Registrants, Foreign Principals, Short Forms.
verdict.notes: Real island-breaker. Foreign-principal file gives COUNTRY + foreign org NAME tied to a US REGISTRANT (firm or individual) - genuine person<->foreign-org<->US-org triple. Encoding is a real gotcha (legacy Latin-1/ISO-8859-1 in older records) - decode explicitly or names mangle. REGISTRATION_NUMBER is the FARA-internal key (no external join), so cross-domain bridging is via NAME/ADDRESS/COUNTRY = fuzzy, not hard. Strong overlap-by-NAME with Senate-LDA registrants is real (many firms file both). ~80k filings; registrant/principal tables are tens of thousands of rows. Use the /bulk/zip/ direct URLs, not the ORDS app param URL.

candidates.name: Congressional Financial Disclosure / STOCK Act PTRs — House Clerk bulk + Senate EFD
candidates.publisher: Clerk of the U.S. House of Representatives / Secretary of the U.S. Senate
candidates.bridges: Ties a named member of Congress to the specific companies (stock TICKERs / asset names) they own and trade; member NAME -> BIOGUIDE (via congress crosswalk) -> the equities they hold
candidates.de_silos: New island -> member NAME joins to congress-legislators (BIOGUIDE) -> FEC; the disclosed TICKER/asset NAME joins to Ripple corporate data (and, once an LEI/CIK normalizer is fed, to SEC EDGAR) — connects legislators to the public companies they regulate and trade
candidates.access: House: annual bulk ZIP of XML index + linked PDFs at disclosures-clerk.house.gov/data (browser-gated, 403 to naive fetch — scrape with headers/Playwright); Senate EFD: per-filer search, PDF + some structured; free, no auth
candidates.volume: Thousands of annual reports + PTRs per Congress; PDFs require parsing for trade-level rows
candidates.creative_join: Conflict-of-interest join: link a member's traded TICKER (PTR) to the companies in Ripple's contractor/lobbying data, then ask 'did this member trade a stock of a firm that lobbied their committee or won a federal contract?' — fuses STOCK Act + LDA + USASpending through one named person

verdict.name: Congressional Financial Disclosure / STOCK Act PTRs — House Clerk bulk + Senate EFD
verdict.access_reality: Confirmed working bulk path: disclosures-clerk.house.gov/public_disc/financial-pdfs/<YEAR>FD.zip, containing <YEAR>FD.xml index (FilingType=P flags PTRs) plus linked PDFs at /public_disc/ptr-pdfs/<YEAR>/<DocID>.pdf. Daily refresh, free, no auth, coverage 2008+. ADVERSARIAL CONFIRMATION of the candidate's own warning: WebFetch on the disclosures-clerk landing page returned HTTP 403 to a naive request - the site is browser-gated and needs real User-Agent headers (or Playwright) to fetch. Senate EFD is per-filer search, mostly PDF.
verdict.notes: Exists and is the only source that ties a NAMED member to specific TICKERs they trade - but the XML index gives only filing metadata (member, filing type, doc id), NOT trade-level rows. Actual TICKER/ASSET_NAME/transaction data lives inside the PDFs (and PTRs are often handwritten-scanned, OCR-hostile). So TICKER as a structured join key is real in principle but requires heavy PDF parsing per the candidate's own note - not a clean field. 403 browser-gate confirmed. BIOGUIDE is NOT in the disclosure files - you join member NAME to congress-legislators to GET bioguide, an extra fuzzy hop. Medium priority is right.

candidates.name: FollowTheMoney.org (Institute on Money in Politics / OpenSecrets) — Entity API
candidates.publisher: OpenSecrets (merged National Institute on Money in Politics + Center for Responsive Politics)
candidates.bridges: Provides a standardized Entity ID that resolves messy donor/employer NAME strings into one entity — the de-duplication layer FEC raw lacks; economic-interest codes group donors by industry, and entity records cross-reference FEC and OpenSecrets IDs
candidates.de_silos: Sits on top of FEC bulk + STATE campaign-finance data (which Ripple lacks entirely) and resolves donor NAME/EMPLOYER to a clean entity that bridges to Ripple corporate NAME carriers and to congress-legislators via FEC/OpenSecrets IDs
candidates.access: Free REST API after creating a myFollowTheMoney account (API key from account export links); JSON/CSV export; rate-limited per account
candidates.volume: State + federal contributions, billions of records aggregated; queried via API, not bulk dump
candidates.creative_join: The standardized EMPLOYER->economic-interest mapping is the creative bridge: it converts raw donor employer text into a clean org entity, dramatically raising the hit rate of donor->company joins against Ripple's NAME backbone — and it is the ONLY source here that adds 50-state campaign data, extending the people<->money graph below the federal level

verdict.name: FollowTheMoney.org (Institute on Money in Politics / OpenSecrets) — Entity API
verdict.access_reality: followthemoney.org/our-data/apis/documentation live. Free API after creating a myFollowTheMoney account; API key passed as URL param (entity.php?eid=...&APIKey=...&mode=xml). XML/CSV/JSON. EXPLICIT MAINTENANCE WARNING on their own docs: 'the site isn't maintained as we integrate with OpenSecrets and you may find bugs' and state data is current only through the 2024 election year - i.e. it is being wound down/merged into OpenSecrets, future of this exact API uncertain. Rate-limited per account.
verdict.notes: The unique value (50-state campaign-finance + a standardized FTM_ENTITY_ID that de-dupes messy donor/employer NAMEs) is real and is the only sub-federal lever in the cluster. BUT: (1) self-declared unmaintained, integrating into OpenSecrets, data frozen at 2024 - aging asset, build defensively; (2) it's an API, not a bulk dump - billions of records are query-only, so you cannot land it wholesale, only pull entities you ask for. The economic-interest-code -> clean-org-entity mapping that boosts donor->company hit rate is genuine and the strongest reason to use it. Treat the API as fragile and possibly being deprecated; OpenSecrets bulk data may be the more durable replacement path.

clusters.thesis: turn the NOAA AIS island (7.3M rows, IMO+MMSI) into an ownership graph
clusters.bridge_thesis: NOAA AIS sits at degree-0 carrying only IMO+MMSI+lat/lon (a "where is it" feed); these owner/registry files map those vessel IDs to a registered owner / manager / operating company that itself carries NAME+ADDRESS (and, via IMO Company Number + sanctions org links, hard entity IDs), converting a position-tracking island into a who-owns-what graph that wires into Ripple's NAME/ADDRESS soft-key layer, foreign company registries, and OFAC.

candidates.name: Equasis vessel + company database
candidates.publisher: Equasis (EMSA / French Ministry of Transport secretariat, ~50 data providers incl. IMO, P&I clubs, class societies, 7 Port State Control regimes)
candidates.bridges: Per-ship: IMO+MMSI -> registered owner NAME + ISM manager NAME + ship manager NAME + their registered ADDRESS + flag. Per-company: company NAME -> full fleet (list of IMOs). This is the IMO/MMSI <-> owner-company crosswalk for ~85,000 ships >100 GT.
candidates.de_silos: Wires NOAA AIS (fed/intl AIS island, 7.3M rows, IMO+MMSI) directly to Ripple's NAME (432 carriers) and ADDRESS (422 carriers) soft-key layer, and onward to the island foreign company registries (Swiss Zefix, Spanish BORME, Greek GEMI, Irish CRO) by owner-company name/address.
candidates.access: Free web account (email + name + address + role); no public API or bulk download — scrape behind login. Playwright/requests-session. Bellingcat documents the scrape pattern. Rate-limit politely; per-IMO and per-company pages.
candidates.volume: ~85,000 vessels in service >100 GT + their owner/manager companies; daily-ish provider feeds.
candidates.creative_join: Owner-company name+address is the bridge OFF the vessel ID and INTO general corporate identity — one Equasis owner record can land a tanker's AIS track next to a Cyprus/Marshall Islands shell in a foreign registry, and (matched on name) next to the same shell in OpenSanctions. Turns 'two ships in the same lane' into 'two ships owned by the same address.'

verdict.name: Equasis vessel + company database
verdict.access_reality: Free web account (email + name + address + role), no public API or bulk download. Per-ship and per-company pages must be scraped behind login. Equasis ToS explicitly forbids storing data in a retrieval system / bulk transmission without written permission, so the warehouse load is technically feasible (Bellingcat + the rhinonix/equasis-cli tool document the scrape pattern) but contractually gray. Rate-limit politely; expect captcha/throttling on bulk pull. Owner/manager NAME + ADDRESS are present per ship; MMSI is present alongside IMO.
verdict.notes: Real, free, and the single best IMO/MMSI<->owner-company crosswalk for ~85k ships. Confirmed it carries registered owner, ISM manager, ship manager + their addresses, plus IMO, MMSI, flag, callsign. Caveat (not drop): the ToS prohibition on storing/redistributing means this is a scrape-behind-login under restrictive terms, not an open dataset. Fine for internal Library use; do NOT republish raw Equasis records. De-silo claim holds: owner name+address bridges AIS island -> NAME/ADDRESS soft-key layer -> foreign registries/OpenSanctions.

candidates.name: OpenSanctions Maritime export (vessels + IMO-bearing organizations)
candidates.bridges: Single CSV containing every sanctioned VESSEL (IMO+MMSI+flag+aliases) AND every ORGANIZATION in OpenSanctions that has been assigned an IMO company number, with the vessel->owning-organization links. Bridges vessel ID to the OpenSanctions entity graph (which separately carries persons, addresses, and other registry IDs).
candidates.de_silos: Wires NOAA AIS + the existing OFAC IMO source (2 sanctioned tankers, currently near-island) into the full OpenSanctions shadow-fleet / PEP / criminal-entity graph. Massively expands the 2-edge IMO sanctions surface.
candidates.access: Direct CSV download (~3.5 MB), rebuilt daily (note timestamped artifact path — fetch latest via the dataset page https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/maritime/). Free under CC-BY-NC 4.0 for non-commercial; commercial needs license. Also available via API.
candidates.volume: Thousands of sanctioned vessels + all IMO-assigned orgs across 401 sources; ~3.5 MB CSV.
candidates.creative_join: Cross-ID bridge: the ORGANIZATION rows tie an IMO company number to entity names/aliases that elsewhere in OpenSanctions resolve to persons and addresses — so a vessel's AIS track can chain IMO -> owning org -> sanctioned individual, jumping the maritime silo into the people/sanctions silo in one hop.

verdict.name: OpenSanctions Maritime export (vessels + IMO-bearing organizations)
verdict.access_reality: The exact timestamped artifact URL in the candidate (20260627150303-gug/maritime.csv) is LIVE and current as of today 2026-06-27, ~3.7 MB, rebuilt daily. Better practice: resolve the latest path via https://data.opensanctions.org/datasets/latest/maritime/index.json rather than hardcoding the timestamp. Anonymous direct download works (no auth, no technical paywall). Confirmed it carries vessels (IMO+MMSI+flag+aliases) AND all organizations assigned an IMO company number, with vessel->org links. Dataset = 291,540 entities / 86,219 targets.
verdict.notes: License is CC-BY-NC 4.0: free for non-commercial (matches Ripple's investigative/portfolio use), commercial requires a paid license. The 'for bulk data clients' phrasing in OpenSanctions' marketing is licensing language, not a technical gate. De-silo claim holds and is high-value: the org rows tie IMO company numbers to entity names/aliases that resolve elsewhere in OpenSanctions to persons + addresses, chaining IMO->owning org->sanctioned individual. Note 'partially_loaded' in Ripple is plausible (existing 2-tanker OFAC IMO source); this massively expands that surface.

candidates.name: IMO GISIS — Ship and Company Particulars
candidates.bridges: Maps ship IMO number -> the IMO Unique Company and Registered Owner Identification Number (a SEPARATE permanent hard ID per managing company/owner) + company name + flag. Introduces a brand-new hard ID type (IMO Company Number) into Ripple that the AIS island can hang on.
candidates.de_silos: Wires NOAA AIS to a company-level hard ID; the same IMO Company Number appears in Equasis and EMSA records, so GISIS is the authoritative spine that lets vessel-owner companies be deduped across the other maritime sources rather than fuzzy-matched on name.
candidates.access: Free public module after email registration; web UI search by IMO (no documented bulk API) — scrape behind login. Some modules are registered-government-only, but Ship & Company Particulars is in the public module.
candidates.volume: Global fleet >100 GT + all registered IMO companies/owners.
candidates.creative_join: Adds a NEW join token (IMO Company Number) to the tagger — a steel-tier company key that is currently entirely absent from the warehouse, turning fuzzy owner-name matches across Equasis/THETIS/OpenSanctions into exact company joins.

verdict.name: IMO GISIS — Ship and Company Particulars
verdict.access_reality: Free public module after IMO web-account registration; web UI search by IMO/name. No documented bulk API -> scrape behind login. Underlying data is supplied to IMO under the IHS Maritime / IMO Secretariat Shipping Information Agreement, so redistribution terms may be restrictive (similar caution to Equasis). Some GISIS modules are government-only, but Ship & Company Particulars is in the public module.
verdict.notes: Confirmed it exposes the IMO Unique Company and Registered Owner Identification Number (permanent per-company/owner hard ID, 100GT+ international fleet) mapped to ship IMO + company name + flag. This is the strongest part of the cluster strategically: it introduces a genuinely NEW steel-tier company key (IMO Company Number) absent from the warehouse, and that SAME number is the join field THETIS and Equasis use for company performance -> turns fuzzy owner-name matching into exact joins. Adding the token to the tagger is the real lever. Verdict solid on existence/access/key; the only watch-item is redistribution terms on raw GISIS records.

candidates.name: USCG PSIX / CGMIX (Port State Information Exchange)
candidates.bridges: Crosswalks IMO <-> USCG Official Number <-> Hull Identification Number (HIN) <-> state registration number <-> call sign for US-documented and certain state-registered vessels, plus inspection/deficiency history. A multi-ID Rosetta Stone for the US-flag fleet.
candidates.de_silos: Wires NOAA AIS (a US/NOAA dataset — strong domestic overlap) to the US vessel-documentation ID space; the Official Number/HIN keys bridge AIS into any US state boating/registration or marine-casualty (NTSB) data Ripple holds or scouts.
candidates.access: Free public web search + a legacy SOAP/REST PSIX web service (no auth) that returns vessel particulars; scrape or call the service. NOTE: Managing-owner name/address was removed from public view in 2018, so this carries vessel-ID crosswalk but NOT current owner PII.
candidates.volume: Hundreds of thousands of US-documented + state-registered vessels.
candidates.creative_join: Even without owner PII, the IMO<->Official Number<->HIN crosswalk lets an AIS track join to NTSB marine-casualty dockets and state registration files — bridging the maritime island into the legal/safety DOCKET silo (2,223 docket edges) on US vessels.

verdict.name: USCG PSIX / CGMIX (Port State Information Exchange)
verdict.access_reality: Free public web search AND a real no-auth SOAP/XML web service at https://cgmix.uscg.mil/xml/psixdata.asmx (PSIXData) — call VesselSummary to get a Vessel Id, then VesselParticulars/etc. Clean programmatic path, no scraping needed, no key. Search supports vessel name, documentation/official number, state reg number, call sign, IMO, and HIN -> confirms the multi-ID crosswalk. CONFIRMED: managing-owner name + address were stripped from public PSIX in 2018, so this carries the vessel-ID Rosetta Stone but NOT current owner PII.
verdict.notes: Best access story in the cluster (genuine public web service, no auth, daily-updated). Crosswalk IMO<->USCG Official Number<->HIN<->state reg<->call sign confirmed for US-documented vessels. Correctly flagged as no-owner-PII (medium priority because the de-silo value is the ID-crosswalk + casualty/registration bridge, not corporate ownership). MMSI presence is weaker than IMO/Official-Number/HIN here — verify the MMSI field coverage at load time, but the steel-grade US crosswalk IDs are solidly present.

candidates.name: ITU MARS — Maritime mobile Access and Retrieval System (Ship Stations)
candidates.bridges: The authoritative MMSI registry: MMSI <-> call sign <-> ship name <-> flag <-> Inmarsat numbers, sourced from member-state administrations (incl. FCC for US). The Rosetta Stone for the MMSI half of the AIS feed.
candidates.de_silos: Many NOAA AIS rows carry MMSI but a stale/absent IMO; MARS resolves MMSI -> ship name + flag so those AIS records can then be matched into the IMO-keyed owner sources (Equasis/GISIS). It rescues the MMSI-only fraction of the 7.3M AIS rows that would otherwise stay island.
candidates.access: Free online query system, updated daily; ship-station search at https://www.itu.int/online/mms/mars/ship_search.sh. No bulk download for full DB (member-state restrictions) — programmatic per-MMSI queries / scrape.
candidates.volume: Global registered ship stations (hundreds of thousands of MMSIs).
candidates.creative_join: MMSI is the only ID many small/fishing/coastal vessels carry; MARS lets those connect at all. Cross-references with FCC ship-station licenses (US side) — bridging MMSI into the FCC/US-licensing silo via call sign.

verdict.name: ITU MARS — Maritime mobile Access and Retrieval System (Ship Stations)
verdict.access_reality: Free online query system, daily-updated, access to MARS and most of its info is unrestricted. Ship-station search returns MMSI <-> call sign <-> ship name <-> flag (List V, ~1 million ship stations from member-state administrations). NO bulk download of the full DB (member-state restrictions) -> programmatic per-MMSI queries / scrape only. Per-record HTSH query endpoint exists (itu.int/online/mms/mars/ship_search.sh).
verdict.notes: Authoritative MMSI registry confirmed. Strategic value is real: resolves the MMSI-only fraction of the 7.3M NOAA AIS rows (small/fishing/coastal vessels that carry MMSI but stale/absent IMO) -> ship name + flag, enabling downstream match into IMO-keyed owner sources. Caveat baked into access (no bulk, per-MMSI scrape = slow at AIS scale), but it does carry the claimed keys and is genuinely public. Medium priority is right. Inmarsat-number coverage is partial; treat call sign + flag as the reliable bridge fields.

candidates.name: EMSA THETIS / Paris MoU Port State Control inspection results
candidates.publisher: European Maritime Safety Agency (EMSA) / Paris MoU
candidates.bridges: Maps ship IMO -> company (owner/manager) name + flag + classification society + full inspection/detention/deficiency history across 27 Paris-MoU states. Ties vessel ID to the responsible company AND a behavioral track record.
candidates.de_silos: Wires NOAA AIS to the European inspection regime: an AIS track that calls a Paris-MoU port gets a company name + detention record. The company-name field joins back to Equasis owner records and the foreign company registry islands.
candidates.access: Free public inspection-results search (Paris MoU also publishes at parismou.org); web UI / scrape, no documented bulk API. THETIS-MED covers the Mediterranean MoU.
candidates.volume: Hundreds of thousands of inspection records on the international fleet calling EU/Paris-MoU ports.
candidates.creative_join: Detention/deficiency history is a risk-signal layer NOT in any other Ripple source — lets a published AIS map of a vessel's movements be overlaid with 'this ship+company has N detentions', bridging maritime tracking into an accountability/enforcement narrative.

verdict.name: EMSA THETIS / Paris MoU Port State Control inspection results
verdict.access_reality: Free public THETIS portal (portal.emsa.europa.eu/web/thetis) for inspection results + a company-performance search; Paris MoU also publishes inspection search at parismou.org. Web UI / scrape, no documented bulk API. THETIS-MED covers the Mediterranean MoU. Company performance is computed against the ISM company and — confirmed in EMSA docs — keyed on the IMO Company Number, which reinforces the GISIS spine.
verdict.notes: Confirmed: maps ship IMO -> responsible company name + flag + class society + inspection/detention/deficiency history across Paris-MoU states. Carries IMO and company NAME; the company-performance layer is indexed by IMO Company Number (steel-tier join to GISIS/Equasis, not just fuzzy name). De-silo + creative-join claims hold: adds a detention/risk-signal layer that exists in no other Ripple source and bridges AIS tracking into an enforcement/accountability narrative. Medium priority appropriate; access is scrape-only (no bulk export).

candidates.name: Global Fishing Watch — Vessel Identity / Vessels API
candidates.bridges: Pre-matched AIS-to-registry crosswalk: GFW's matching process pairs AIS identity (MMSI, call sign, name) to public regional/national vessel registries on IMO+MMSI+name+flag, emitting a reconciled vessel-identity record with flag, gear type, and registry provenance.
candidates.de_silos: Directly the same identity space as NOAA AIS (both AIS-derived) — GFW supplies the IMO<->MMSI<->registry reconciliation that NOAA AIS lacks, so it's the natural enrichment layer that turns raw AIS MMSIs into registry-confirmed vessels.
candidates.access: Free API with token (register for API key); REST + R package (gfwr). Vessel-identity datasets also downloadable. Non-commercial / research terms.
candidates.volume: Hundreds of thousands of AIS-tracked vessels, multi-year identity history.
candidates.creative_join: GFW resolves AIS identity spoofing/gaps (vessels that switch MMSI) — the dedup it provides prevents the AIS island from fragmenting one real ship into many nodes when wired to the owner sources.

clusters.thesis: open the aviation vertical (TAIL/ICAO24<->owner) — the Epstein-plane spine
clusters.bridge_thesis: Ripple has zero aviation key today; the FAA MASTER file simultaneously carries TAIL (N-Number), ICAO24 (Mode-S hex), registrant NAME and full ADDRESS — one bulk file mints the entire aviation key space AND immediately wires every aircraft to Ripple's existing NAME/ADDRESS-keyed corporate, EIN and person islands, turning the 7.3M-row NOAA AIS island and the scouted OpenSky island into a connected graph.

candidates.name: FAA Releasable Aircraft Database (MASTER + ACFTREF + ENGINE + DEALER + DOCINDEX)
candidates.publisher: U.S. Federal Aviation Administration, Aircraft Registry (Oklahoma City)
candidates.bridges: TAIL<->ICAO24 (Mode-S hex, pos 602-606 base16) in one row, plus TAIL->registrant NAME+ADDRESS+county(FIPS). This is the canonical N-Number<->Mode-S crosswalk for the entire US fleet. ACFTREF joins MFR/MODEL code; OTHER NAMES fields carry co-owners.
candidates.de_silos: Mints the TAIL and ICAO24 tokens that the engine has no data for today. Wires the NOAA AIS-style aviation islands: directly keys OpenSky (scouted) and ADS-B Exchange by ICAO24, and bridges every aircraft into Ripple's NAME (432 carriers) and ADDRESS (422 carriers) soft-key surface — landing it next to corporate registries, EIN/IRS-990, SAM.gov entities.
candidates.access: Bulk ZIP download, ~60MB, no auth, free, public-domain US-gov data. Refreshed daily 23:30 Central. Fixed-width/comma-delimited .txt files documented in ardata.pdf (registry.faa.gov/database/ardata.pdf). FAA web pages 403 to scrapers but the direct ZIP and ardata.pdf URLs resolve.
candidates.volume: ~300,000+ active US civil aircraft records (MASTER), plus full ACFTREF make/model reference table.
candidates.creative_join: TYPE REGISTRANT code (pos 57: 1=Individual, 3=Corporation, 7=LLC, 8=Non-Citizen Corp, 9=Non-Citizen Co-Owned) + registrant NAME + street ADDRESS lets you fuzzy-join an aircraft to a corporate entity or named person in Ripple's corp/EIN/person data — the creative owner-name->entity bridge, not just same-key matching. Shared addresses cluster shell-LLC fleets.

verdict.name: FAA Releasable Aircraft Database (MASTER + ACFTREF + ENGINE + DEALER + DOCINDEX)
verdict.access_reality: Free, no-auth, public-domain US-gov bulk download. Direct ZIP at registry.faa.gov/database/ReleasableAircraft.zip (~60MB, comma-delimited .txt files, refreshed daily). FAA HTML pages 403 to scrapers (confirmed: both faa.gov and registry.faa.gov returned HTTP 403 to my fetches) but the direct ZIP and ardata.pdf URLs resolve for non-browser clients. Official landing page: faa.gov/licenses_certificates/aircraft_certification/aircraft_registry/releasable_aircraft_download. No rate limits on a single daily ZIP pull. Mature Python tooling exists (PyPI faa-aircraft-registry).
verdict.notes: MASTER file confirmed to carry the 'MODE S CODE' and 'MODE S CODE HEX' (=ICAO24, base16) field alongside N-Number/registration, registrant NAME, street ADDRESS, county, and TYPE REGISTRANT (1=Individual/3=Corp/7=LLC/8=Non-Citizen Corp). This is the canonical US TAIL<->ICAO24 crosswalk and mints both tokens the engine currently has no data for. The owner-NAME/ADDRESS columns bridge directly to Ripple's NAME(432)/ADDRESS(422) soft-key surface. Highest-value aviation load by a wide margin.

candidates.name: OpenSky Network Aircraft Database (aircraftDatabase.csv monthly snapshots)
candidates.bridges: icao24 -> registration (tail) -> owner, operator, operatoricao, operatoriata, built, model. Crucially carries explicit owner AND operator name strings keyed to ICAO24 globally (not just US), filling in non-US tails the FAA file can't.
candidates.de_silos: Global ICAO24<->registration crosswalk that extends the FAA US-only spine worldwide; lets the warehouse resolve any ICAO24 seen in NOAA AIS-style ADS-B feeds to a tail and owner. Wires directly to the FAA MASTER (US overlap by ICAO24) and to NAME-keyed corporate data via owner/operator strings.
candidates.access: Free CSV download, monthly snapshots preserved; full DB at opensky-network.org/data/aircraft. No auth for the metadata CSV (the live API needs a free account). File exceeds 10MB (could not WebFetch in one shot) but resolves and downloads fine via requests.
candidates.volume: ~500,000+ airframe rows globally; updated irregularly per OpenSky's own note.
candidates.creative_join: operator/owner free-text + operatoricao gives a second, independent owner attribution to cross-check against FAA registrant name — disagreements flag trustee/shell structures (e.g. FAA shows 'Bank of Utah Trustee' while OpenSky shows the real operator).

verdict.name: OpenSky Network Aircraft Database (aircraftDatabase.csv monthly snapshots)
verdict.access_reality: Free CSV, NO account needed for the metadata file (the account requirement is only for the live state-vector API). Two working URLs: opensky-network.org/datasets/metadata/aircraftDatabase.csv and s3.opensky-network.org/data-samples/metadata/aircraftDatabase.csv; dated monthly snapshots (e.g. aircraftDatabase-2021-04.csv) preserved. File >10MB so single-shot WebFetch fails but plain requests download is fine. Aggregated from official+community sources, updated irregularly.
verdict.notes: Columns confirmed: icao24, registration, manufacturericao, manufacturername, model, typecode, serialnumber, operator, operatorcallsign, operatoricao, operatoriata, owner, built, registered, modes, adsb, etc. Carries explicit owner AND operator name strings keyed to ICAO24 GLOBALLY, filling non-US tails the FAA file can't. Independent owner attribution to cross-check vs FAA registrant (trustee/shell flag). Strong global extension of the FAA US spine. Note: 'already_in_ripple: scouted_not_loaded' is accurate per warehouse state.

candidates.name: ADS-B Exchange Aircraft Metadata / Basic Aircraft DB (unfiltered, includes LADD/PIA aircraft)
candidates.bridges: Hex (ICAO24) -> registration -> type/operator, plus historical position tracks (lat/lon + timestamp) keyed by ICAO24. The unfiltered network captures aircraft on the FAA LADD/PIA block list that other feeds suppress.
candidates.de_silos: Adds the SPATIAL+TEMPORAL dimension to the aviation spine: ICAO24-keyed flight tracks join to Ripple's LATLON surface (the NOAA AIS island's geographic moat) and let you place an owner's jet at a place/time. Wires ADS-B tracks -> FAA owner -> corporate entity.
candidates.access: Subscription data products (live feed, historical archive, daily flight-events feed); not a free bulk download. The basic readsb aircraft DB JSON is openly mirrored (e.g. github.com/wiedehopf/tar1090-db). Commercial cost + rate limits apply for the full historical archive; format JSON/Parquet.
candidates.volume: Real-time global positions every 250ms; multi-TB historical archive. Aircraft metadata table ~500k airframes.
candidates.creative_join: Because it does NOT honor FAA LADD masking the way FAA-fed vendors must, it bridges blocked tail numbers back to ICAO24 -> FAA registrant — defeating the very privacy program owners use to hide, then chaining to the owner's other Ripple assets.

verdict.name: ADS-B Exchange Aircraft Metadata / Basic Aircraft DB (unfiltered, includes LADD/PIA aircraft)
verdict.access_reality: The high-value part (historical position archive, daily flight-events, live feed) is SUBSCRIPTION-ONLY with minimum annual commitments; vendor explicitly states 'does not offer one-time data extracts or project-based access' and 10-yr backfill is subscribers-only. NOT a free bulk load. The static ICAO24<->reg/type metadata claim is better sourced from the openly-mirrored Mictronics readsb DB (github.com/wiedehopf/tar1090-db, aircraft.csv.gz on the csv branch, free raw GitHub download) -- which is the same data class and free. So the metadata is gettable for free elsewhere, but the spatial/temporal de-silo value that makes this candidate interesting sits behind the paywall.
verdict.notes: ICAO24->position tracks are real and would add the spatial/temporal dimension, but they are paywalled with no project-tier access -- out of scope for a free public-data warehouse. Recommendation: drop ADSBx as a load target; instead load tar1090-db (free, open, ICAO24->reg/type/owner) for the static metadata, and treat live tracks as out-of-budget. The LADD-unfiltered tracking angle is real but only via paid feed.

candidates.name: FAA LADD (Limiting Aircraft Data Displayed) Block List
candidates.bridges: List of tail numbers / call signs / ICAO addresses whose owners requested suppression. By itself a TAIL/ICAO24 list, but as a JOIN it flags WHICH owners in the FAA MASTER actively sought privacy.
candidates.de_silos: Inverts the privacy signal into an accountability signal: LEFT JOIN FAA MASTER on TAIL where LADD-listed = the subset of aircraft owners who paid to hide. Wires straight onto the FAA spine and surfaces high-interest registrants for the corporate/person cross-walk.
candidates.access: FAA-provided list to approved tracking vendors; public-facing request portal at ladd.faa.gov. Programmatic access typically via vendors (e.g. AeroDataBox LADD API). Not a clean open bulk CSV — auth/vendor gated; treat as a derived enrichment flag, not a primary load.
candidates.volume: Thousands of blocked registrations (grew sharply after 2024 FAA Reauthorization Act Sec. 803 made privacy opt-in easier).
candidates.creative_join: A 'who's hiding' overlay: an owner appearing on LADD AND holding aircraft via a Bank-of-Utah-style trust AND linked by address to multiple LLCs is the exact pattern worth surfacing — pure de-anonymization fuel.

verdict.name: FAA LADD (Limiting Aircraft Data Displayed) Block List
verdict.access_reality: Real FAA program (faa.gov/pilots/ladd), codified by FAA Reauthorization Act of 2024 Sec.803 (now opt-in/upon-request). But the actual block LIST is NOT a clean public bulk CSV -- it is distributed to FAA-data vendors who sign agreements to suppress listed tails; the public face is a request portal (ladd@faa.gov / CARES system). Programmatic access is via third-party vendor APIs (e.g. AeroDataBox) which are themselves auth/cost gated. The candidate itself correctly flags 'auth/vendor gated; treat as enrichment, not a primary load.'
verdict.notes: The accountability-inversion idea (LEFT JOIN FAA MASTER to flag who paid to hide) is genuinely valuable, but there is no first-class public bulk source for the list. Treat as a derived enrichment flag if/when a vendor or FOIA-obtained snapshot is available -- not a clean onboard. Volume grew sharply post-2024 Act. Keep as a method, not a primary load.

candidates.name: Bank of Utah Aircraft Owner Trust beneficiary pattern (trustee-of-record signal in FAA MASTER)
candidates.publisher: Bank of Utah Corporate Trust (registrant of record in FAA data) — derived, not a standalone dataset
candidates.bridges: Not a separate file — it's a NAME-pattern bridge inside the FAA MASTER: registrant NAME LIKE '%TRUSTEE%' / 'BANK OF UTAH' / 'WELLS FARGO TRUST' identifies the ~3,000+ aircraft held in owner trusts where FAA shows the trustee, hiding the beneficial owner.
candidates.de_silos: Defines the analytical layer that connects the aviation spine to BENEFICIAL OWNERSHIP. Flags trust-held aircraft so they can be cross-referenced against FinCEN/beneficial-ownership, FARA, OFAC/OpenSanctions (scouted) and foreign company registries (the Zefix/BORME/GEMI/CRO islands) — turning anonymized tails into named principals.
candidates.access: No download; this is a modeling rule over the FAA MASTER (and a known list of the handful of dominant trustees: Bank of Utah, Wells Fargo Trust, TVPX, Aircraft Guaranty/Wright Brothers fame). Beneficiary identity itself requires subpoena per Bank of Utah, so the join is via OTHER NAMES, operator, and shared address.
candidates.volume: Bank of Utah alone: 3,000+ aircraft in FAA owner trusts; the full trustee cohort covers tens of thousands of registrations.
candidates.creative_join: The marquee creative join: trustee-name pattern -> operator/co-owner -> shared address -> the same LLC's UEI/EIN/CIK elsewhere in Ripple. This is how you walk an anonymously-registered jet to a real person — the Epstein-plane methodology generalized.

verdict.name: Bank of Utah Aircraft Owner Trust beneficiary pattern (trustee-of-record signal in FAA MASTER)
verdict.access_reality: Explicitly NOT a standalone dataset -- the candidate says so. It is a modeling/classification rule over the FAA MASTER file (registrant NAME LIKE '%TRUSTEE%' / 'BANK OF UTAH' / 'WELLS FARGO TRUST' / 'TVPX' / 'AIRCRAFT GUARANTY'). Beneficial-owner identity behind the trust is NOT public (Bank of Utah states it requires subpoena). No file to download.
verdict.notes: Drop as a data SOURCE -- there's nothing to load. KEEP as a high-value analytical layer once the FAA MASTER is landed: trustee-name pattern -> OTHER NAMES/operator/shared-address -> walk to LLC's EIN/CIK/UEI elsewhere in Ripple. The Bank-of-Utah '3,000+ trust aircraft' and dominant-trustee cohort are real and well-documented. This is methodology, not a candidate dataset; verdict=drop only in the sense that it's not a source to onboard.

candidates.name: Airframes.org International Aircraft Registry (ICAO24<->registration, multi-country)
candidates.bridges: 987,667 registrations mapped to 805,887 ICAO24 addresses across international registries — the broadest ICAO24<->registration crosswalk, covering non-US prefixes (HZ-, P4-, M-, VP-, 9H-) that the FAA file and OpenSky miss.
candidates.de_silos: Extends the ICAO24 spine to foreign-registered aircraft, the exact tails that route through offshore trust structures. Connects ICAO24-keyed ADS-B sightings to foreign registrant names, which then bridge to the foreign company registry islands (Zefix/BORME/GEMI/CRO) via NAME.
candidates.access: Web lookup + community add-info portal; bulk access is informal (no clean public bulk endpoint, scrape-required, attribution/community terms). Treat as supplementary coverage for non-US tails rather than a primary load.
candidates.volume: 622,433 airframes / 987,667 registrations / 805,887 ICAO24 addresses (self-reported).
candidates.creative_join: Fills the non-US gap so an ICAO24 with no FAA/OpenSky hit still resolves to a registration and country — the missing edge that keeps internationally-registered jets from being islands.

verdict.name: Airframes.org International Aircraft Registry (ICAO24<->registration, multi-country)
verdict.access_reality: Database is real and the self-reported counts check out (622,433 airframes / 987,667 registrations / 805,887 ICAO24 addresses / 20,744 operators). BUT the TOS explicitly forbids bulk use: 'It is not allowed to collect and transfer these data anywhere else. Copyright rules apply,' and the site rate-limits lookups and forbids automated/high-transaction usage. No clean public bulk endpoint -- scrape-required and against terms. There is a signup/selfservice portal but it is for contributing/lookups, not bulk export.
verdict.notes: Broadest international ICAO24<->registration coverage (real, would fill non-US tails), but legally/technically not loadable: bulk collection and re-transfer are explicitly prohibited and rate-limited. Drop as a load target. The non-US ICAO24 gap is better filled by the free, openly-licensed Mictronics/tar1090-db CSV and OpenSky's global file, which already cover most foreign prefixes without the TOS problem.

clusters.thesis: activate the huge DOCKET key (2,223 edges) by extracting party identities
clusters.bridge_thesis: Ripple holds the DOCKET key on 115 datasets (2,223 edges) but has never extracted the party identities behind those dockets, so courts sit as a soft "same case" island that never resolves to the corporations, contractors, and healthcare entities those cases are actually about.

candidates.name: CourtListener / RECAP Parties + Dockets API (v4)
candidates.bridges: DOCKET -> party NAME (and attorney NAME). The /parties/ endpoint is filterable by docket id and returns the litigant name for every plaintiff/defendant, including corporate/organization names; this is the missing link that turns Ripple's existing docket numbers into named entities that then match NAME->EIN/UEI/NPI carriers already landed.
candidates.de_silos: Wires the entire 115-dataset / 2,223-edge DOCKET cluster (PACER/CourtListener dockets) to the NAME-carrying entity universe (432 NAME-tagged sources) and through it to EIN (IRS 990), CIK (SEC EDGAR), UEI/CAGE (SAM/USASpending), NPI/CCN (CMS) - i.e. it gives the docket island its defendants.
candidates.access: REST API v4, JSON; free API token required (Authorization: Token <token>); generous rate limits for non-commercial; filter ?docket=<id>. Bulk export does NOT include parties/attorneys (PACER-derived), so the API is the only path. Public domain data.
candidates.volume: ~hundreds of millions of PACER-derived items; tens of millions of party records across federal dockets
candidates.creative_join: Resolve a defendant NAME on a docket to its EIN/CIK/UEI, then ask: which companies being sued under the False Claims Act are simultaneously winning new federal contracts (USASpending UEI) or are tax-exempt hospitals (IRS 990 EIN + CMS CCN)? The docket becomes a spine connecting litigation to money and care delivery.

verdict.name: CourtListener / RECAP Parties + Dockets API (v4)
verdict.access_reality: Endpoint is real: https://www.courtlistener.com/api/rest/v4/parties/ filterable by ?docket=<id>, returns party NAME (incl. corporate/org names) with nested attorney NAME (use filter_nested_results=True). DOCKET->NAME bridge confirmed. ACCESS CHANGED May 2026 (free.law/2026/05/07): the old 'every user gets 5,000 req/hr' is gone. New users get LOW default rate limits unless they buy a membership; only pre-existing heavy users (1,000+ requests) grandfathered; EDU membership is free with a generous tier. Token auth (Authorization: Token <token>) still required. Confirmed parties/attorneys are NOT in the bulk export (bulk has dockets/opinions/judges/financial-disclosures but no parties), so the API really is the only path - and that path is now metered/paid for volume. PACER-derived data, public domain.
verdict.notes: Bridge is real and high-value (gives the 2,223-edge DOCKET cluster its named defendants). Caveat: the candidate's 'free token, generous rate limits' is outdated as of May 2026 - serious volume needs a paid CourtListener membership (or free EDU tier). Plan a token + rate-limit budget; tens of millions of party records means this is a long metered crawl, not a one-shot bulk pull. Resolved NAME still needs fuzzy matching to land on EIN/CIK/UEI - it's a NAME bridge, not a hard-ID-on-the-row bridge.

candidates.name: FAPIIS / Responsibility-Qualification Records (SAM.gov Data Bank)
candidates.bridges: Carries UEI + CAGE + legal business NAME on the SAME record as criminal/civil/administrative PROCEEDINGS (incl. case identifiers, terminations for cause, suspension/debarment). This is a true crosswalk: it ties a litigation/proceeding directly to a hard contractor ID (UEI), no name-matching required.
candidates.de_silos: Connects the courts/proceedings world directly to the federal-contractor backbone - wires to USASpending (UEI 1 edge today, wide open), SAM entity management, and the DOCKET cluster. Turns 'company was sued' into 'this exact UEI was sued and still holds contracts.'
candidates.access: SAM.gov Data Bank extracts (Responsibility/Qualification reports); searchable by legal name, UEI, or CAGE; 14-day public posting delay. Bulk extract download via SAM.gov Data Bank; some entity-management endpoints require a free SAM API key.
candidates.volume: Tens of thousands of proceeding/integrity records, refreshed continuously
candidates.creative_join: FAPIIS is the rare file where UEI and a litigation proceeding live in one row - it lets Ripple cross debarred/litigated contractors against active USASpending awards (same UEI) to find entities being paid by the government while under integrity proceedings.

verdict.name: FAPIIS / Responsibility-Qualification Records (SAM.gov Data Bank)
verdict.access_reality: Confirmed: FAPIIS migrated into SAM.gov as 'Responsibility/Qualification' records (former fapiis.gov). SAM.gov Data Bank offers TWO downloadable static reports - entity basics + R/Q (integrity) records, and entity basics + proceedings records. R/Q data AND proceedings are also exposed via the Entity Management API v3 at open.gsa.gov. Records key on UEI (and CAGE) and carry legal business NAME on the same row as criminal/civil/administrative proceedings - a genuine hard-ID-on-the-row crosswalk (UEI<->proceeding), no name-matching required. 14-calendar-day public posting delay confirmed. Entity Management API requires a free SAM.gov API key (system-account/role-based); bulk Data Bank extracts are the simpler public path.
verdict.notes: Best candidate in this cluster for de-siloing: it puts UEI + proceeding in one record, directly wiring the courts/integrity world to the federal-contractor backbone (USASpending UEI, SAM entity mgmt). This is exactly the (A) Rosetta-Stone shape the task prioritizes. Minor friction: SAM API key onboarding has historically been fiddly (role-based access), so prefer the Data Bank bulk extract for first load.

candidates.name: DOJ News / Press Releases API (api_v1)
candidates.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Office of Public Affairs
candidates.bridges: Each release carries defendant/settling-party NAME in the body plus structured COMPONENT (e.g. Civil Division) and TOPIC (e.g. False Claims Act) fields, frequently citing the case DOCKET number. Filterable by component+topic to isolate enforcement/settlement releases, giving NAME<->DOCKET<->settlement-amount per action.
candidates.de_silos: Bridges the courts/enforcement narrative layer to entity NAME carriers; directly enriches the FED_DOJ_FCA_SETTLEMENTS island and the DOCKET cluster by naming the settling companies and tying them to dollar amounts and statutes.
candidates.access: Public REST API, JSON and XML, no auth/API key; filter by date, component, topic; field selection via ?fields=. ~14,000+ press releases, speeches, blog entries.
candidates.volume: ~14,000+ items, updated daily
candidates.creative_join: Mine FCA/healthcare-fraud releases for defendant names, resolve to NPI/CCN (CMS) or EIN (IRS), and rank which Medicare-billing providers in Ripple's dense NPI/CCN graph have a matching DOJ settlement - turning the healthcare join surface into a fraud-exposure map.

verdict.name: DOJ News / Press Releases API (api_v1)
verdict.access_reality: Confirmed real and public: https://www.justice.gov/api/v1/press_releases.json - no auth/API key, JSON and XML, filter by date/component/topic, field selection supported. ~14,000+ items (press releases, speeches, blogs). REALITY CHECK on volume: default 20 results/request, MAX 50/request (pagesize=50), and >4 requests/sec triggers throttling/blocking - so a full pull is a paginated, rate-limited crawl, not a bulk dump. KEY CAVEAT: NAME and DOCKET are confirmed to live in the unstructured release BODY text, not as discrete structured fields; component/topic ARE structured. So NAME<->DOCKET<->amount extraction requires NLP/regex on body text, not a clean join column.
verdict.notes: Genuinely useful for enriching the FED_DOJ_FCA_SETTLEMENTS island and naming settling parties, but it's a narrative/text source - budget for entity extraction. carries_claimed_key='likely' because the keys are present in prose, not as fields. Pair component=Civil Division + topic=False Claims Act to isolate enforcement actions.

candidates.name: SEC Enforcement / Litigation Releases (structured)
candidates.publisher: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (litigation releases) / sec-api.io for parsed
candidates.bridges: Litigation releases name defendant entities and, when matched to a public company, carry CIK (and ticker). This is a NAME->CIK bridge on enforcement actions - exactly the hard ID Ripple is starving for (CIK 1 edge, 2 carriers today).
candidates.de_silos: Connects SEC EDGAR (CIK universe) to the DOCKET/enforcement cluster - activating Ripple's idle CIK normalizer. Wires enforcement to the public-company filing graph.
candidates.access: Free official litigation releases (HTML/scrape, 1995-present, ~10k+ releases). Structured CIK-tagged version via sec-api.io (paid API, POST JSON). Official SEC data is public domain.
candidates.volume: 10,000+ litigation releases since 1995
candidates.creative_join: Tie an SEC fraud defendant's CIK to its EDGAR insider filings (already-scouted SEC insiders) and its government contracts (if the same parent carries a UEI) - a public company's enforcement record cross-referenced with who was selling shares around the action.

verdict.name: SEC Enforcement / Litigation Releases (structured)
verdict.access_reality: Official SEC litigation releases exist (https://www.sec.gov/enforcement-litigation/litigation-releases, 1995-present, ~10k+) but are HTML-only - NO official structured/JSON feed and NO CIK in the official release text. SEC.gov actively blocks automated fetch (returned HTTP 403 on a plain request; requires a declared User-Agent per SEC fair-access policy, rate-limited ~10 req/sec). The CIK/ticker-tagged structured version is ONLY via sec-api.io, a PAID third-party (confirmed: ticker/CIK fields are 'optional, only present if name matched to a known public company'). So the claimed NAME->CIK bridge does NOT exist in the public/official data - CIK is added by the paid parser or must be derived yourself.
verdict.notes: carries_claimed_key='refuted' for the FREE/official path: CIK is NOT in sec.gov litigation releases - it's value-added by the paid sec-api.io parser. Free path is 'scrape HTML (with proper User-Agent) + fuzzy-match names to EDGAR CIK yourself.' Source itself is real and public; the structured CIK bridge as described is paywalled or DIY. Medium priority is right; don't expect a clean CIK column for free.

candidates.name: CFTC Enforcement Actions (via OpenSanctions)
candidates.bridges: Respondent entity/person NAME + CFTC docket number, delivered as reconciled structured entities (FollowTheMoney) with OpenSanctions entity IDs - a NAME normalization layer that can be matched against Ripple's NAME-carrying corporate sources.
candidates.de_silos: Bridges commodity/derivatives enforcement to the entity NAME graph; complements the SEC/FTC enforcement bridges to give a near-complete federal financial-regulator enforcement spine over the DOCKET cluster.
candidates.access: OpenSanctions free tier: bulk JSON/CSV (FtM) download + reconciliation API; some volume tiers paid. CFTC source is public.
candidates.volume: Thousands of respondents/actions
candidates.creative_join: Cross CFTC respondents against SEC defendants (both via OpenSanctions NAME reconciliation) to find entities hit by multiple regulators, then bridge to CIK/UEI - a multi-agency 'repeat offender' surface no single docket exposes.

verdict.name: CFTC Enforcement Actions (via OpenSanctions)
verdict.access_reality: Confirmed dataset: https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/us_cftc_enforcement_actions/ - 1,195 entities (the candidate's 'thousands' is high; it's ~1.2k). Bulk download in both simplified tabular (CSV) and detailed FollowTheMoney JSON, refreshed daily, with OpenSanctions canonical entity IDs and reconciliation API. Respondent NAME + CFTC action confirmed as reconciled structured entities. LICENSE CAVEAT: free for non-commercial/personal use only; commercial use requires a paid OpenSanctions data license - relevant given the freelance/commercial ceiling goal.
verdict.notes: Clean, small, structured - the FtM reconciliation layer is the value (de-dupe CFTC respondents against SEC defendants by canonical entity). Solid for a NAME-reconciliation bridge. Watch the commercial-use license if this ever feeds a paid product. ~1.2k entities, low volume, easy load.

candidates.name: FTC Cases & Proceedings (Legal Library)
candidates.bridges: Each case carries respondent company NAME, FTC matter/docket number, and federal-court docket - giving NAME<->DOCKET for antitrust and consumer-protection respondents, with industry context that maps to NAICS.
candidates.de_silos: Bridges FTC enforcement to the entity NAME universe and, via NAICS, into Ripple's largest soft-key cluster (NAICS 6,441 edges); ties merger/antitrust respondents to the corporate graph.
candidates.access: Official Legal Library (HTML, scrape; case title, matter number, docket, federal court, filing date). FTC dev API (api.ftc.gov/v0) is limited to DNC/HSR endpoints, so cases require scraping. Public domain.
candidates.volume: Thousands of cases/proceedings, decades of history
candidates.creative_join: Join FTC merger respondents (NAME) to USASpending contractors (UEI) and SEC filers (CIK) to surface companies that were both consolidating markets and feeding from federal contracts at the same time.

verdict.name: FTC Cases & Proceedings (Legal Library)
verdict.access_reality: Legal Library is real and public (https://www.ftc.gov/legal-library/browse/cases-proceedings) with case title, matter/docket number, federal court, filing date in the page data. Confirmed: NO official FTC API for cases - the FTC dev API (api.ftc.gov) only covers DNC/HSR, so cases MUST be scraped. Third-party scrapers (e.g. Apify) exist and confirm extractable fields (title, status, matter number, docket, court, date, summary, press releases), corroborating that NAME<->DOCKET is present, but only via HTML scrape. Public domain.
verdict.notes: Real source, real NAME<->DOCKET, but scrape-only - no API, so build/maintain a scraper (or pay for Apify). NAICS mapping is the candidate's inference, not a field in the data (no NAICS on FTC cases), so that bridge is derived not native. carries_claimed_key='likely' for NAME/DOCKET (present in scraped HTML); the NAICS claim is weak. Medium priority is fair.

candidates.name: EOIR Immigration Court Case Data (Deportation Data Project, processed FOIA)
candidates.bridges: Pipe-delimited bulk CSVs (~30GB, 18 linkable tables) keyed on EOIR case/docket IDs with court location (geocodable to FIPS). It massively expands the DOCKET cluster's row count and adds a geographic spine, though parties are largely individuals (low corporate-ID bridge value).
candidates.de_silos: Connects immigration-court dockets to the geographic backbone (court FIPS) and to detention-facility / institution datasets; broadens the DOCKET key footprint beyond federal civil litigation.
candidates.access: Free bulk download (CSV, Stata, SPSS, feather) from Deportation Data Project; raw monthly FOIA dumps from DOJ EOIR FOIA Library. No auth.
candidates.volume: One row per removal case since FY1998; tens of millions of records across 18 tables
candidates.creative_join: Geo-join EOIR court load (by FIPS) against detention-facility operators and their contractor UEIs to map immigration-court caseload to the private firms paid to detain - a courts-to-contractor bridge built on geography, not party name.

clusters.thesis: the ADDRESS bridge — property/parcel data linking owners<->LLCs<->physical assets
clusters.bridge_thesis: Property/deeds data turns ADDRESS and OWNER-NAME from coarse glue into a real shell-detection surface: a registered LLC owner resolves to Ripple's corporate registries (EIN/CIK/foreign), and co-address clustering links shells that share a registered-agent or mailing address — and HMDA specifically injects LEI, the one normalizer Ripple has with ~zero data.

candidates.name: CFPB/FFIEC HMDA — Panel + Transmittal Sheet (lender file) + LAR
candidates.bridges: The HMDA Panel/Transmittal Sheet is a true Rosetta Stone: it carries LEI <-> RSSD ID <-> Tax ID (EIN) <-> legal name + address for every mortgage lender. One file wires LEI to EIN to NAME for thousands of financial entities.
candidates.de_silos: This is THE lever for Ripple's idle LEI normalizer (normalizer exists, ~zero LEI data). It wires LEI into the warehouse for the first time and ties those LEIs to EIN — connecting to the IRS 990/EO-BMF and SEC EDGAR EIN-carrying islands, and to GLEIF if/when landed. LAR loan rows carry FIPS+census-tract geo, joining to the ZIP(5,541)/FIPS soft-key mass.
candidates.access: Bulk download, no auth, free. Combined nationwide LAR + per-year panel/TS as CSV-in-ZIP. No rate limits on file downloads. Note: CFPB stopped producing the consolidated Reporter Panel after 2024 release — use 2018-2023 panel files plus annual transmittal sheets for the LEI<->RSSD<->TaxID crosswalk.
candidates.volume: LAR ~15-25M loan rows/year; panel/TS ~5-7K institutions/year
candidates.creative_join: Lender LEI on a mortgage + the loan's property census tract lets you tie a specific financial institution (LEI->EIN->corporate registry) to the exact geography of the lending — and bridge ADDRESS-keyed property records to a hard-ID'd lender. It is the cleanest on-ramp to populate LEI across the graph.

verdict.name: CFPB/FFIEC HMDA — Panel + Transmittal Sheet (lender file) + LAR
verdict.access_reality: Fully public, free, no auth. Bulk combined nationwide LAR + per-year panel/TS as CSV at ffiec.cfpb.gov (Data Publication + Data Browser); historic panel zips at files.consumerfinance.gov. No rate limits on file downloads. CFPB documentation confirms the Panel data fields include LEI, tax_id, respondent_rssd, respondent_name, parent_rssd, top_holder_rssd, and the TS carries LEI + federal tax ID + name + address per filer. The candidate's caveat is REAL and important: beginning with the 2024 release (June 2025) CFPB stopped producing the consolidated Reporter Panel — use 2018-2023 panel files + annual TS for the LEI<->RSSD<->TaxID crosswalk.
verdict.notes: This is a genuine Rosetta Stone and the cleanest on-ramp to wire Ripple's idle LEI normalizer to EIN + RSSD + NAME for thousands of lenders. Every claimed key confirmed against CFPB's own field docs. LAR rows carry FIPS/census-tract geo (joins to ZIP/FIPS soft-key mass). Highest-value candidate in this cluster. One nuance: the lender 'tax ID' is the institution's EIN, which bridges to IRS BMF/SEC EIN islands as claimed.

candidates.name: NYC ACRIS — Real Property Parties (+ Master + Legals)
candidates.publisher: NYC Department of Finance (via NYC Open Data / Socrata)
candidates.bridges: Parties (46.4M rows: document_id + party_type + NAME + ADDRESS) joins to Legals (document_id -> BBL = Borough-Block-Lot parcel) and Master (document_id -> deed/mortgage type, amount, date). document_id is the shared key chaining buyer/seller name+address to a physical parcel and a transaction.
candidates.de_silos: Wires NYC parcels into Ripple's NAME(432) and ADDRESS(422) carrier mass and turns coarse address glue into entity-grade shell detection. Party NAME that is an LLC resolves to corporate registries; mailing ADDRESS clustering reveals shells sharing a registered-agent. Connects to PLUTO (below) via BBL.
candidates.access: Socrata SODA API + bulk CSV, no auth (app token recommended for throughput), free. ~46.4M party rows; paginate via $limit/$offset or grab the full CSV export.
candidates.volume: Parties 46.4M rows; Master ~17M; Legals ~17M
candidates.creative_join: Group Parties by normalized mailing ADDRESS to surface LLC clusters all registered to the same agent/PO box — the classic anonymous-shell-LLC fingerprint — then resolve each LLC NAME to NY corporate registries and chase the deed (Master) for purchase price and date. ADDRESS becomes the bridge from a physical building to a hidden ownership network.

verdict.name: NYC ACRIS — Real Property Parties (+ Master + Legals)
verdict.access_reality: Public Socrata (SODA) API + bulk CSV, no auth required (app token recommended for throughput, not mandatory). Verified live row count on 636b-3b5g = 46,375,598 (matches the claimed 46.4M exactly). document_id + party_type + name + address columns confirmed. Master (bnx9-e6tj) and Legals (8h5j-fqxa) dataset IDs are real and live. document_id chains Parties->Legals(BBL)->Master(transaction).
verdict.notes: Confirmed real, public, and free at the claimed scale. The BBL join to PLUTO is real (Legals carries BBL). Strong shell-LLC detection play via mailing-address clustering on the NAME/ADDRESS carrier mass. NYC-only scope is the only limit, not a defect.

candidates.name: NYC PLUTO / MapPLUTO (Primary Land Use Tax Lot Output)
candidates.bridges: Keyed on BBL (Borough-Block-Lot) for 870K+ NYC lots; carries OwnerName + building/land-use attributes + lat/lon. BBL is the join to ACRIS Legals, so PLUTO's owner-of-record name attaches directly to the ACRIS deed chain.
candidates.de_silos: Completes the NYC parcel triangle: PLUTO (current owner + physical building, by BBL) <-> ACRIS Legals (BBL <-> document_id) <-> ACRIS Parties (document_id <-> transacting NAME/ADDRESS). Plugs into ADDRESS/NAME/ZIP/FIPS carrier mass and gives every building a lat/lon for spatial join to anything geographic.
candidates.access: Bulk CSV/Shapefile/GeoJSON, no auth, free, monthly updates. ~870K rows.
candidates.volume: ~870K tax lots, 80+ attributes each
candidates.creative_join: PLUTO's OwnerName field is frequently an LLC. Cross PLUTO owner-name against ACRIS shell clusters and against corporate registries to map which physical buildings a hidden owner network actually controls — physical asset <-> shell <-> registry, all bridged through BBL and ADDRESS.

candidates.name: Regrid Nationwide Parcel Data (Snowflake-delivered, standardized owner schema)
candidates.bridges: 160M parcels across 3,229 counties under ONE standardized schema (v16): standardized owner name, owner mailing address, deeded owner, situs address, parcel geometry, all keyed by county FIPS + a persistent Regrid UUID. Also ships an optional Regrid-ID <-> Overture GERS-ID bridge for premium bulk customers.
candidates.de_silos: Single biggest force-multiplier on Ripple's ADDRESS(422) + NAME(432) carriers: it nationalizes the NYC parcel pattern to the whole US under one schema, so owner-name LLC resolution and co-mailing-address shell clustering work coast-to-coast and join to corporate registries everywhere. Delivers natively to Snowflake.
candidates.access: Paid (per-county/state/national bulk license OR Parcel API; auth required). Formats: Parquet/CSV/SQL/Shapefile/GeoPackage via S3, SFTP, or direct Snowflake share. No free public tier for bulk; free trial/preview only.
candidates.volume: ~160M parcels nationwide
candidates.creative_join: Standardized owner mailing-address lets you cluster shell LLCs that hold property in DIFFERENT counties/states but share one mailing address or agent — invisible to any single-county source. That cross-jurisdiction co-address cluster is the de-silo move that single-city portals can't make.

candidates.name: FinCEN Residential Real Estate (RRE) Reporting Rule — beneficial-ownership dataset
candidates.bridges: Reports tie a transferee ENTITY/TRUST (name, address, tax ID/EIN) on an all-cash residential transfer to its individual BENEFICIAL OWNERS (>=25% equity) by name + address — the single most direct LLC-to-human bridge that exists for property.
candidates.de_silos: If/when any portion becomes accessible (currently law-enforcement/regulatory access, not public bulk — track for FOIA/aggregate releases), it directly connects shell-LLC property buyers to named humans and to EIN, wiring property ADDRESS clusters to Treasury/FinCEN islands (FDIC enforcement, OpenSanctions) and to corporate registries.
candidates.access: NOT public bulk today: reporting persons file to FinCEN; data is restricted-access. Watch for aggregate/statistical releases or FOIA. Mark for monitoring, not immediate load.
candidates.volume: New as of 2026 (rule in effect ~Mar 2026); volume unknown, ramping
candidates.creative_join: The only public-adjacent source that closes the last mile: shell LLC -> 25% beneficial owner human name+address. Even aggregate releases let you validate address-clustering shell hypotheses built from ACRIS/Regrid against ground-truth beneficial ownership.

verdict.name: FinCEN Residential Real Estate (RRE) Reporting Rule — beneficial-ownership dataset
verdict.access_reality: Two killers. (1) The data was NEVER public — FinCEN stores RRE reports in a secure non-public database, access limited to federal/state law enforcement + qualifying financial institutions. No bulk, no FOIA path to record-level beneficial-owner data. (2) The rule was VACATED IN ITS ENTIRETY by the U.S. District Court for E.D. Texas on 2026-03-19 (Flowers Title Companies, LLC v. Bessent) for APA violation / exceeding BSA authority; FinCEN has stated reporting persons are not currently required to file while the order stands. So there is effectively no dataset being collected right now, let alone a public one.
verdict.notes: The candidate already rated it low/monitor-only, which was the right instinct, but it's now worse than 'restricted' — the underlying rule is judicially vacated as of March 2026, so the collection itself is paused. Not a data source. Revisit only if the vacatur is reversed on appeal AND aggregate/statistical releases ever appear (neither has happened). Drop from any near-term plan.

candidates.name: EWG Farm Subsidy Database + USDA FSA Name/Address Payment Files
candidates.publisher: Environmental Working Group (FOIA from USDA FSA/NRCS)
candidates.bridges: Every subsidy/conservation recipient since 1995 by recipient NAME + ADDRESS + custnumber, plus FSA's official Name/Address payment files. Many recipients are farm LLCs/partnerships — name+address resolves to corporate registries and to land ownership.
